title: MultiEarth 2022 Deforestation Challenge -- ForestGump
title_zh: 多地球 2022 森林砍伐挑战——阿甘正传
abstract: The estimation of deforestation in the Amazon Forest is challenge task because of the vast size of the area and the difficulty of direct human access. However, it is a crucial problem in that deforestation results in serious environmental problems such as global climate change, reduced biodiversity, etc. In order to effectively solve the problems, satellite imagery would be a good alternative to estimate the deforestation of the Amazon. With a combination of optical images and Synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images, observation of such a massive area regardless of weather conditions become possible. In this paper, we present an accurate deforestation estimation method with conventional UNet and comprehensive data processing. The diverse channels of Sentinel-1, Sentinel-2 and Landsat 8 are carefully selected and utilized to train deep neural networks. With the proposed method, deforestation status for novel queries are successfully estimated with high accuracy.
summary: The estimation of deforestation in the Amazon Forest is challenge task because of the vast size of the area and the difficulty of direct human access. However, it is a crucial problem in that deforestation results in serious environmental problems such as global climate change, reduced biodiversity, etc. In order to effectively solve the problems, satellite imagery would be a good alternative to estimate the deforestation of the Amazon. With a combination of optical images and Synthetic aperture radar (SAR) images, observation of such a massive area regardless of weather conditions become possible. In this paper, we present an accurate deforestation estimation method with conventional UNet and comprehensive data processing. The diverse channels of Sentinel-1, Sentinel-2 and Landsat 8 are carefully selected and utilized to train deep neural networks. With the proposed method, deforestation status for novel queries are successfully estimated with high accuracy.
abstract_zh: 由于亚马逊森林面积广阔且人类直接进入的困难，对亚马逊森林砍伐情况的估计是一项具有挑战性的任务。然而，森林砍伐导致了全球气候变化、生物多样性减少等严重的环境问题，这是一个至关重要的问题。为了有效解决这些问题，卫星图像将是估算亚马逊森林砍伐情况的一个很好的替代方案。通过将光学图像和合成孔径雷达 (SAR) 图像相结合，无论天气条件如何，都可以对如此大的区域进行观测。在本文中，我们提出了一种利用传统 UNet 和综合数据处理的准确森林砍伐估计方法。 Sentinel-1、Sentinel-2 和 Landsat 8 的不同通道经过精心挑选并用于训练深度神经网络。利用所提出的方法，成功地高精度地估计了新查询的森林砍伐状态。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于UNet和多源卫星图像的亚马逊森林砍伐估计方法。
AI.motivation: 亚马逊森林砍伐导致严重环境问题，但传统方法难以覆盖大面积区域，需借助卫星图像与深度学习技术实现高效监测。
AI.method: 结合Sentinel-1/2、Landsat 8的多通道数据，利用UNet深度神经网络模型进行综合数据处理与训练。
AI.result: 成功实现对新型查询区域的高精度森林砍伐状态估计，有效克服天气条件限制。
AI.conclusion: 该方法验证了多源卫星数据与深度学习结合在大规模森林监测中的可行性。

title: A Feature Memory Rearrangement Network for Visual Inspection of Textured Surface Defects Toward Edge Intelligent Manufacturing
title_zh: 用于边缘智能制造纹理表面缺陷视觉检测的特征记忆重排网络
comment: Revision to IEEE transactions on automation science and engineering
comments: Revision to IEEE transactions on automation science and engineering
abstract: Recent advances in the industrial inspection of textured surfaces-in the form of visual inspection-have made such inspections possible for efficient, flexible manufacturing systems. We propose an unsupervised feature memory rearrangement network (FMR-Net) to accurately detect various textural defects simultaneously. Consistent with mainstream methods, we adopt the idea of background reconstruction; however, we innovatively utilize artificial synthetic defects to enable the model to recognize anomalies, while traditional wisdom relies only on defect-free samples. First, we employ an encoding module to obtain multiscale features of the textured surface. Subsequently, a contrastive-learning-based memory feature module (CMFM) is proposed to obtain discriminative representations and construct a normal feature memory bank in the latent space, which can be employed as a substitute for defects and fast anomaly scores at the patch level. Next, a novel global feature rearrangement module (GFRM) is proposed to further suppress the reconstruction of residual defects. Finally, a decoding module utilizes the restored features to reconstruct the normal texture background. In addition, to improve inspection performance, a two-phase training strategy is utilized for accurate defect restoration refinement, and we exploit a multimodal inspection method to achieve noise-robust defect localization. We verify our method through extensive experiments and test its practical deployment in collaborative edge--cloud intelligent manufacturing scenarios by means of a multilevel detection method, demonstrating that FMR-Net exhibits state-of-the-art inspection accuracy and shows great potential for use in edge-computing-enabled smart industries.
summary: Recent advances in the industrial inspection of textured surfaces-in the form of visual inspection-have made such inspections possible for efficient, flexible manufacturing systems. We propose an unsupervised feature memory rearrangement network (FMR-Net) to accurately detect various textural defects simultaneously. Consistent with mainstream methods, we adopt the idea of background reconstruction; however, we innovatively utilize artificial synthetic defects to enable the model to recognize anomalies, while traditional wisdom relies only on defect-free samples. First, we employ an encoding module to obtain multiscale features of the textured surface. Subsequently, a contrastive-learning-based memory feature module (CMFM) is proposed to obtain discriminative representations and construct a normal feature memory bank in the latent space, which can be employed as a substitute for defects and fast anomaly scores at the patch level. Next, a novel global feature rearrangement module (GFRM) is proposed to further suppress the reconstruction of residual defects. Finally, a decoding module utilizes the restored features to reconstruct the normal texture background. In addition, to improve inspection performance, a two-phase training strategy is utilized for accurate defect restoration refinement, and we exploit a multimodal inspection method to achieve noise-robust defect localization. We verify our method through extensive experiments and test its practical deployment in collaborative edge--cloud intelligent manufacturing scenarios by means of a multilevel detection method, demonstrating that FMR-Net exhibits state-of-the-art inspection accuracy and shows great potential for use in edge-computing-enabled smart industries.
abstract_zh: 纹理表面工业检查（以目视检查的形式）的最新进展使得高效、灵活的制造系统的此类检查成为可能。我们提出了一种无监督特征记忆重排网络（FMR-Net）来同时准确地检测各种纹理缺陷。与主流方法一致，我们采用了背景重建的思想；然而，我们创新地利用人工合成缺陷来使模型能够识别异常，而传统智慧仅依赖于无缺陷样本。首先，我们采用编码模块来获取纹理表面的多尺度特征。随后，提出了基于对比学习的记忆特征模块（CMFM）来获得判别性表示并在潜在空间中构建正常特征记忆库，该记忆库可以用作补丁级别的缺陷和快速异常分数的替代品。接下来，提出了一种新颖的全局特征重排模块（GFRM）来进一步抑制残留缺陷的重建。最后，解码模块利用恢复的特征来重建正常的纹理背景。此外，为了提高检测性能，采用两阶段训练策略来进行精确的缺陷恢复细化，并利用多模态检测方法来实现抗噪声缺陷定位。我们通过大量实验验证了我们的方法，并通过多级检测方法测试了其在协同边缘云智能制造场景中的实际部署，证明FMR-Net具有最先进的检测精度，并在边缘计算智能工业中显示出巨大的应用潜力。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种无监督特征记忆重排网络（FMR-Net），用于在边缘智能制造场景中检测纹理表面缺陷。
AI.motivation: 传统纹理表面缺陷检测依赖无缺陷样本，难以适应复杂工业场景。需开发高效、灵活的视觉检测方法以满足智能制造需求。
AI.method: 通过编码模块提取多尺度特征，结合对比学习记忆模块（CMFM）构建正常特征库，并利用全局特征重排模块（GFRM）抑制残余缺陷，最终通过解码模块重建正常背景。采用两阶段训练策略和多模态检测方法提升鲁棒性。
AI.result: 实验验证FMR-Net在多个数据集上达到最先进水平，并成功部署于边缘-云端协同制造场景，展现高检测精度与实用性。
AI.conclusion: FMR-Net通过创新网络结构与训练策略，有效解决纹理缺陷检测难题，为边缘计算驱动的智能工业提供可行方案。

ai_content.tldr: 本文提出一种无监督特征记忆重排网络（FMR-Net），用于在边缘智能制造场景中检测纹理表面缺陷。
ai_content.motivation: 传统纹理表面缺陷检测依赖无缺陷样本，难以适应复杂工业场景。需开发高效、灵活的视觉检测方法以满足智能制造需求。
ai_content.method: 通过编码模块提取多尺度特征，结合对比学习记忆模块（CMFM）构建正常特征库，并利用全局特征重排模块（GFRM）抑制残余缺陷，最终通过解码模块重建正常背景。采用两阶段训练策略和多模态检测方法提升鲁棒性。

ai_content.conclusion: FMR-Net通过创新网络结构与训练策略，有效解决纹理缺陷检测难题，为边缘计算驱动的智能工业提供可行方案。

search_text: A Feature Memory Rearrangement Network for Visual Inspection of Textured Surface Defects Toward Edge Intelligent Manufacturing 用于边缘智能制造纹理表面缺陷视觉检测的特征记忆重排网络 Recent advances in the industrial inspection of textured surfaces-in the form of visual inspection-have made such inspections possible for efficient, flexible manufacturing systems. We propose an unsupervised feature memory rearrangement network (FMR-Net) to accurately detect various textural defects simultaneously. Consistent with mainstream methods, we adopt the idea of background reconstruction; however, we innovatively utilize artificial synthetic defects to enable the model to recognize anomalies, while traditional wisdom relies only on defect-free samples. First, we employ an encoding module to obtain multiscale features of the textured surface. Subsequently, a contrastive-learning-based memory feature module (CMFM) is proposed to obtain discriminative representations and construct a normal feature memory bank in the latent space, which can be employed as a substitute for defects and fast anomaly scores at the patch level. Next, a novel global feature rearrangement module (GFRM) is proposed to further suppress the reconstruction of residual defects. Finally, a decoding module utilizes the restored features to reconstruct the normal texture background. In addition, to improve inspection performance, a two-phase training strategy is utilized for accurate defect restoration refinement, and we exploit a multimodal inspection method to achieve noise-robust defect localization. We verify our method through extensive experiments and test its practical deployment in collaborative edge--cloud intelligent manufacturing scenarios by means of a multilevel detection method, demonstrating that FMR-Net exhibits state-of-the-art inspection accuracy and shows great potential for use in edge-computing-enabled smart industries. 纹理表面工业检查（以目视检查的形式）的最新进展使得高效、灵活的制造系统的此类检查成为可能。我们提出了一种无监督特征记忆重排网络（FMR-Net）来同时准确地检测各种纹理缺陷。与主流方法一致，我们采用了背景重建的思想；然而，我们创新地利用人工合成缺陷来使模型能够识别异常，而传统智慧仅依赖于无缺陷样本。首先，我们采用编码模块来获取纹理表面的多尺度特征。随后，提出了基于对比学习的记忆特征模块（CMFM）来获得判别性表示并在潜在空间中构建正常特征记忆库，该记忆库可以用作补丁级别的缺陷和快速异常分数的替代品。接下来，提出了一种新颖的全局特征重排模块（GFRM）来进一步抑制残留缺陷的重建。最后，解码模块利用恢复的特征来重建正常的纹理背景。此外，为了提高检测性能，采用两阶段训练策略来进行精确的缺陷恢复细化，并利用多模态检测方法来实现抗噪声缺陷定位。我们通过大量实验验证了我们的方法，并通过多级检测方法测试了其在协同边缘云智能制造场景中的实际部署，证明FMR-Net具有最先进的检测精度，并在边缘计算智能工业中显示出巨大的应用潜力。 本文提出一种无监督特征记忆重排网络（FMR-Net），用于在边缘智能制造场景中检测纹理表面缺陷。 Haiming Yao,Wenyong Yu,Xue Wang cs.CV,cs.AI
